danger_signals.meaning: Apex threat level; supernatural danger; world-ending scenario

danger_signals.meaning: Hidden threat; escalating danger depth; trap detection needed

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary world equilibrium; breathing room before new crisis

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stable settlements; active towns; normal civilization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs at rest; normal trade; peaceful exploration; elderly survivors (Guy, Artea) living quietly

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress of Doom reappears in sky; dark magical aura; environmental corruption spreading
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kingdoms under attack; military mobilization; party formation; survivors fleeing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple fortress phases; underwater temple revealed; environmental hazards increasing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party seeks weapons (Dual Blade); recruitment of allies; desperate battles; time pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character model/visual change for Lufia; Sinistral corruption aesthetic applied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party member defection to enemy; betrayal moment; combat shift (Lufia becomes hostile)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortress interior at collapse; Sinistrals fused into monstrous final form; catastrophic environmental effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All-or-nothing battle; Lufia redeemable through combat; Fortress destruction animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World returned to peace; settlements rebuilt; Fortress gone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lufia returns without memories; protagonist chooses domestic life; world healing

environmental_storytelling.example: Fortress of Doom's massive floating island and dark castle structure visually communicate it as world-threatening; scale far exceeds normal structures
Churches mark safe zones and story progression; Sinistral locations (fortress, underwater temple) marked by dark aesthetics and dangerous dungeons
Collapsed fortress after first victory becomes underwater ruins; fortress re-emergence signals failed peace; final collapse signals world restoration
Northern kingdom under siege visually demonstrates Sinistral threat level; besieged towns show immediate danger compared to peaceful interior settlements
Elderly survivor NPCs (Guy, Artea) visually indicate long peace period; their involvement signals crisis severity; deaths mark escalation
Dual Blade sword serves as visual link between past war (Maxim era) and current conflict; weapon's Sinistral origin foreshadows betrayal

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Playing in fields; exploration-focused; responding to events
Leading party into dungeons; commanding turn-based battles; strategic decision-making
Traveling with party; fighting alongside protagonist; emotional support presence
Joins Sinistrals; becomes enemy combatant; dual-nature conflict visible in party dynamics
Leading military attacks on kingdoms; territorial aggression; demonstrating power
Emerging from dormancy; staging coordinated attacks; testing party strength; setting traps
Transforming into monstrous entity; all-or-nothing combat engagement; desperation attack patterns
Passing weapons to next generation (bow to Jerin); mentoring; accepting mortality
Dies upon learning Sinistrals have returned; trauma response to resurrection threat
Trading; offering services; stable settlement routines
Fleeing; seeking shelter; expressing fear; military mobilization

notes: Lufia & the Fortress of Doom communicates world-state primarily through environmental scale (Fortress of Doom size), location aesthetics (dark vs. peaceful), NPC behavior changes, and narrative checkpoints. Danger escalation is visualized through Sinistral manifestations, environmental destruction, and fortress reappearance. Factions are identified through location association (Sinistrals=dark fortress/underwater temple, player party=towns/churches) and character alliances. The game employs temporal storytelling (99-year peace marker) to enhance crisis impact when Sinistrals return. The Dual Blade serves as a visual/mechanical bridge between past and present conflicts, enhancing narrative coherence. Lufia's identity crisis is a key world-state indicator—her transformation signals fundamental threat to party stability. Environmental destruction (fortress collapse, chasm formation) directly maps to combat outcomes, creating visual feedback for world-state changes.
